raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A section review centrally includes checking whether the argument moves coherently from observed bias to calibration results to the final trustworthiness claim.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims about bias elimination, agreement, model families, and judge tasks, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims are supported by the reported experiments.","R1.5":"Reviewing a section includes assessing whether the paragraph order and narrative arc effectively present motivation, method, evidence, and takeaway.","R1.6":"A generic section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, register, and flow.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended because the delegation is brief.","RX.2":"The review responsibility must avoid endorsing claims such as complete elimination or broad generalization beyond the evidence shown.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than rewriting, editing, or extending the artifact.","RX.4":"The review should avoid guidance that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unreliable evaluation practices.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibility should be traceable to the wording of the delegation and the claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally includes checking whether the argument moves coherently from observed bias to calibration results to the final trustworthiness claim.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about bias elimination, agreement, model families, and judge tasks, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims are supported by the reported experiments.
rationale.R1.5: Reviewing a section includes assessing whether the paragraph order and narrative arc effectively present motivation, method, evidence, and takeaway.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, register, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended because the delegation is brief.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility must avoid endorsing claims such as complete elimination or broad generalization beyond the evidence shown.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than rewriting, editing, or extending the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid guidance that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unreliable evaluation practices.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should be traceable to the wording of the delegation and the claims present in the artifact.
